question: Where should the new {{target}} be added relative to existing {{context}}?
plain_language_preface: We're adding {{target}} to the system. Placement affects dependency order, test isolation, and rollback blast radius.

options.label: Prepend — add before existing entries
options.tradeoff: Simplest rollback (delete head); may shift indices.

options.label: Append — add after existing entries
options.tradeoff: No index shift; rollback = delete tail.

options.label: Inline — insert at logical dependency position
options.tradeoff: Correct ordering; harder rollback if mid-list.

options.label: Parallel — add as sibling in new namespace/module
options.tradeoff: Zero blast radius to existing; requires callers to opt-in.

options.label: Skip add — use existing entry with a flag

recommended_rationale: Parallel placement has zero blast radius and supports incremental opt-in; switch to C only when ordering is load-bearing.
